message:
feat(writing): define "skill" upfront, link jsm.md, swap hero illustration

Three post-publish fixes per reader feedback:

1. Add a paragraph defining what an AI "skill" is — a bundle of
   "how to" knowledge prepared for use by an AI agent such as Claude
   Code or OpenAI's Codex, combining reference materials, workflows,
   scripts, and subagents in an agent-intuitive, agent-ergonomic,
   context-efficient form. Most readers will have never heard the
   term; grounding it once up front avoids jargon-blindness through
   the rest of the piece.

2. Link "my paid skills site" to jeffreys-skills.md with an explicit
   anchor and rel=noopener,noreferrer. Hoists the brand into the
   early narrative rather than leaving it ambient.

3. Swap the hero illustration for a fresh 1536x1024 composition,
   cwebp q=92 (~334KB). Keeps the same filename so no import
   changes required.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -576,9 +576,31 @@ export function WillsEstateArticle() { and writing down your answers.

+

+ Before I go further, a note for anyone who has not heard the word + before: a skill can be thought of as a bundle of + “how to” knowledge that is prepared for use by an AI + agent such as Claude Code or OpenAI's Codex. It combines + reference materials, workflows, scripts, subagents, and so on, all + in a way that is maximally agent-intuitive and agent-ergonomic and + optimized for context efficiency (at least for a well-designed + skill, which most are not). The agent loads the skill at the start + of a session and then behaves like a prepared specialist for the + rest of that conversation. +

+

A few months ago I posted a tax-preparation skill on my paid skills - site. At least five readers have since told me they saved more than + site,{" "} + + jeffreys-skills.md + + . At least five readers have since told me they saved more than a thousand dollars on their taxes using it. One saved twenty thousand. That is on a twenty-dollar-a-month subscription. It got me thinking about which other expensive-specialist problems look
